widgets.name: ActivityTimeline
widgets.description: Shows ordered steps as a vertical list or a horizontal progress bar.

props.steps: TimelineStep[] (status, title, optional subtitle, action, badge per step)

usage: Use for onboarding, KYC, or checkout steps where the user should see progress through a sequence.
Use horizontal layout for compact progress-only UI; vertical when steps need subtitles, actions, or badges.
notes: Horizontal mode has no per-step action button—only labels and a fill bar.
Vertical per-step actions use a native `<button>`, not the Button component.
Do not use for arbitrary lists without a step metaphor; use ListItem or cards instead.

widgets.name: AllocationBreakdownWidget
widgets.description: Implemented widget: section title (optional SectionHeader) plus rows of label, formatted value, percent, and a token-styled bar; supports loading and empty copy.

props.title: string
props.rows: AllocationBreakdownRow[] — each { label: string, valueLabel: string, percent: number (0–100), optional trend?: positive | negative | neutral }

usage: Use on portfolio drill-downs, MF holdings, goal or risk summaries whenever the spec shows class/category mix with amount and share.
Use inside `.sh-section` + `.sh-section__content` (or equivalent) after performance or holdings blocks.
Use when bars are proportional to percent; for two or three KPIs only, consider DataPoints instead.
notes: Source: `src/components/AllocationBreakdownWidget/` — export `AllocationBreakdownWidget`, types `AllocationBreakdownRow`, `AllocationBreakdownWidgetProps`.
Prefer this widget over reassembling SectionHeader + Card + ListItem + bar markup so spacing, separators, and tokens stay consistent for generated screens.
Bar fill width is `percent` clamped to 0–100; format `valueLabel` with INR rules (no space after ₹).
`showRowTrend` and `row.trend` exist on the API but are not rendered yet—do not rely on visible sentiment until implemented.

widgets.name: AmountSelectionWidget
widgets.description: Payment amount step: choose a predefined option (e.g. minimum due, total due) or enter a custom amount with inline helper and validation, plus optional primary CTA readiness.

props.title: string — section title (e.g. Choose amount)
props.options: AmountOption[] — each { id: string, label: string }
props.selectedOptionId: string | null — which preset is active; null or sentinel when custom path is active
props.customAmount: string — controlled raw input for custom entry
props.customAmountLabel: string — floating label for custom field (e.g. Amount (INR))
props.helperText: string — rules or limits shown next to custom input (e.g. max outstanding)
props.validationMessage: string — inline error; empty when valid
props.currency: string — e.g. INR for copy/format hints
props.showCustomAmount: boolean — whether custom TextField is visible (true when custom option selected)
props.ctaEnabled: boolean — mirrors whether Continue/Pay step may proceed
props.summaryContent: ReactNode — optional recap when not in custom mode (e.g. bold amount + suffix)

usage: Prefer AmountSelectionWidget on bill pay, wallet top-up, or loan payment flows immediately after the bill summary (or equivalent) step.
Use when the product offers both preset amounts and a custom entry—avoid hand-wiring Chip rows and TextField separately without shared validation state.
Source: `src/components/AmountSelectionWidget/` — parent owns the primary CTA; keep `ctaEnabled` in sync with validation when wiring Continue/Pay outside the widget.
notes: Source: `src/components/AmountSelectionWidget/` — export `AmountSelectionWidget`, types `AmountOption`, `AmountSelectionWidgetProps`.
**Predefined vs custom** paths must be explicit: `selectedOptionId` and `showCustomAmount` must stay in sync with the visible UI (no hidden custom value while a preset appears selected).
**Helper text** and **validation** must appear in the **same visual group** as the custom input, not only after submit or on another screen.
**ctaEnabled** (or equivalent derived state) must reflect validity—do not enable the step CTA when `invalid` or when custom entry is incomplete.
Prefer this contract over ad hoc **chips + loose input** so transactional flows pass `screen-review-checklist.md` (custom amount + CTA checks).

widgets.name: BillSummaryWidget
widgets.description: Bill or card payment summary block at the start of a pay flow: card identity, bill/payment status, total due, minimum due, outstanding, due date, and statement period in a clear hierarchy.

props.cardName: string — product/display name (e.g. card tier or product label)
props.issuer: string — optional bank or network label
props.billStatus: string — short status (e.g. Payment due, No bill yet)
props.totalDue: string — formatted INR, statement total due
props.helperText: string — optional assistive line under amounts
props.statusNote: string — optional banner-style note (due soon, autopay on, etc.)
props.partialFailureMessage: string — when bill snapshot failed partially; omit or empty when OK
props.onRetryFetch: () => void — optional when partialFailureMessage is set

usage: Prefer BillSummaryWidget as the first content block after flow chrome on credit-card bill pay, utility bill pay, or similar checkout-adjacent flows.
Use when the spec requires total due, minimum due, outstanding, and due date together—avoid one-off Card + loose Text stacks.
Source: `src/components/BillSummaryWidget/` — maps `statusNote` to a sleek Alert; only one `billStatus` badge today (multi-chip dashboards may need an extension).
notes: Source: `src/components/BillSummaryWidget/` — export `BillSummaryWidget`, type `BillSummaryWidgetProps`.
**Financial hierarchy** must be obvious: lead with what the user owes now vs minimum vs total; do not bury due date or outstanding.
**Amount groupings** must not look interchangeable—use labels, order, and emphasis so total due, minimum due, and outstanding are never visually ambiguous.
Prefer this widget contract over ad hoc **Card + text** composition so generated screens stay aligned with `screen-generation-rules.md` payment UX and INR formatting (no space after ₹).

widgets.name: BottomNav
widgets.description: Fixed bottom tab bar with icons, labels, and optional home indicator.

props.items: { icon: string; activeIcon?: string; label: string }[]

usage: Use as the single primary navigation for a phone-width shell (typically one per screen root).
Use when switching top-level destinations, not for in-screen filters (use Chip or SegmentedControl).
notes: Pin to the bottom of the viewport; pair with solid `--surface-level-1` per design system (no blur unless spec says so).
Prefer ≤5 items; controlled when `activeIndex` is provided.
Do not nest a second BottomNav inside scroll content.

widgets.name: DataPoints
widgets.description: Two- or three-column KPI row with labels, main value, and optional subtitle.
usage: Use inside cards or advisory rows to summarize two or three numbers side by side.
Use when alignment matters (split ends vs centered triple)—pick `type` to match the design.
notes: `type=3` requires exactly three items; two-column types require two.
Do not use for more than three metrics in one row; add another DataPoints block or a different pattern.
Prefer this over hand-placing three text stacks when spacing must match the design system.

widgets.name: FlowStepperWidget
widgets.description: Canonical step-progress UI for multi-step transactional flows (bill pay, checkout, KYC, setup): maps step metadata to completed, current, upcoming, and blocked visuals with optional horizontal or vertical orientation.

props.steps: FlowStep[] — each { id: string, label: string, optional shortLabel?: string }
props.currentStepId: string — must match the visible screen step
props.completedStepIds: string[] — prior completed step ids; when every id is listed, all steps render completed (e.g. processing/success)
props.status: default | loading | disabled — widget-level chrome (e.g. dim while submitting)
props.onStepPress: (stepId: string) => void — optional; not wired in ActivityTimeline wrapper yet
props.ariaLabel: string — progress region description (maps to root aria-label)

usage: Prefer FlowStepperWidget over manually composing circles, connector lines, and labels for payment, checkout, KYC, or onboarding step chrome.
Use when the screen plan defines explicit step ids and the current route or step state must stay in sync with the progress UI.
Source: `src/components/FlowStepperWidget/` — thin wrapper over `ActivityTimeline`; steps are only completed vs awaiting (no distinct “current” visual).
notes: Source: `src/components/FlowStepperWidget/` — export `FlowStepperWidget`, types `FlowStep`, `FlowStepperWidgetProps`, `FlowStepperStatus`.
The **current** step id must always match the **visible** screen; never show a future step as complete.
Label and state mapping must be **explicit** in props (`currentStepId`, `completedStepIds`)—do not infer completion from array order alone without validation.
Prefer this registry contract over ad hoc progress rows so AI-generated flows stay consistent with `screen-generation-rules.md` (flow accuracy) and `screen-review-checklist.md` (Flow Accuracy).

widgets.name: GraphWidget
widgets.description: Portfolio-style line chart: gradient stroke, glow layers, and endpoint marker.
usage: Use inside PortfolioWidget or another hero when you have an SVG path in the default 346×116 viewBox space.
Use when you need this exact glow/endpoint treatment—not as a general-purpose charting solution.
notes: Path must end at the documented endpoint; see GraphWidget.tsx comments.
Optional tuning: height, viewBox, indicatorXRatio, showGradient, showRipple, showDot—omit unless you know coordinates.
Do not use without a valid `svgPath`; this is not a data-to-path chart library.

widgets.name: Header
widgets.description: Top app chrome: status bar, homepage or titled layout, optional Tabs and Chip row.
usage: Use once at the top of each phone screen before scrollable content.
Use `type=homepage` for discover-style entry; `large`/`regular` for inner pages with back and actions.
notes: Do not place inside `.sh-content` scroll—keep fixed/sticky at top of the 376px column.
Chip row is for global header filters; dense portfolio time ranges belong in PortfolioWidget, not duplicate chip rows here unless spec requires.
Tab props mirror `Tabs` / `TabItem`; align `activeTabValue` with tab `value` strings.

widgets.name: HeatmapWidget
widgets.description: Sector heatmap strip with wheel tabs and per-sector mosaic cards.
usage: Use on Stocks Discover (or similar) for index/stocks/F&O sector performance at a glance.
Use when `wheelItems` keys must map to `sectorsByWheel` datasets.
notes: Prefer this over standalone WheelCarousel + custom mosaics—the layout and tall/short mosaic modes are built in.
`sectors` bypasses wheel switching for a static strip only.
Index/F&O mosaics are taller than Stocks; use `wheelCanonicalTabOrder` if display labels differ from canonical tab ids.

widgets.name: HoldingsSectionWidget
widgets.description: Implemented widget: optional SectionHeader, then Loading, or a vertical stack of StocksCard rows, or an empty Card with copy plus optional primary Button.

props.title: string
props.items: StocksCardProps[] — each row matches **StocksCard** (title, quantity, avgPriceLabel, price, changeLabel, changeSentiment; optional layout mtf, statusLabel, statusBadgeTone, margin*, avatar*, trailing*, onClick)

usage: Use on portfolio drill-downs, watchlists, and position summaries when each row is one security (StocksCard shape).
Place after performance or allocation blocks inside `.sh-section` rhythm; keep stacks in the phone column scroll area.
Do not use for MF fund cards at AMC density—use MFCardWidget; do not use for simple name+LTP strips—compose SectionHeader + Chip + Card + ListItem instead.
notes: Source: `src/components/HoldingsSectionWidget/` — export `HoldingsSectionWidget`, type `HoldingsSectionWidgetProps`; `items` is `StocksCardProps[]`.
Prefer this widget over hand-building SectionHeader + gap stack + StocksCard so empty states, spacing (`--spacing-16` between cards), and INR strings stay consistent for AI-generated screens.
`loading` shows Loading instead of the stack; `items=[]` with `emptyMessage` and optional `emptyCtaLabel` + `onEmptyCtaClick` drives the empty Card.
Per-item fields follow **StocksCard**—pass through optional layout, status, margin, avatar, and trailing props as needed.

widgets.name: LedgerActivityListWidget
widgets.description: Implemented widget: optional SectionHeader, then loading, unavailable plain copy, empty copy, or a Card of default ListItem rows (primary line, subtitle/meta, trailing value).

props.title: string
props.items: LedgerActivityItem[] — each { primaryText: string, secondaryText: string, trailingText: string, optional statusLabel?: string }
props.unavailableMessage: string — plain copy when the list failed to load (e.g. partial fetch); omit Card

usage: Use on portfolio drill-downs, order history, SIP/statement feeds, payments, or any dense event list with amount or delta on the trailing edge.
Place inside `.sh-section` rhythm after holdings or allocation blocks; keep inside the phone column scroll region.
Do not use for step-based flows—use ActivityTimeline. Do not use for news cards—use NewsWidget.
notes: Source: `src/components/LedgerActivityListWidget/` — export `LedgerActivityListWidget`, types `LedgerActivityItem`, `LedgerActivityListWidgetProps`.
Prefer this widget over hand-building SectionHeader + Card + ListItem stacks so separators, subtext, and INR trailing strings stay consistent for AI-generated screens.
When `unavailableMessage` is non-empty, it renders instead of the Card (partial fetch). `loading` replaces the list with Loading.
`statusLabel` on items is accepted but not rendered yet—do not rely on visible per-row status until implemented.

widgets.name: MFCardWidget
widgets.description: Mutual fund summary card with AMC avatar, optional tags, metrics, and footer.
usage: Use in MF home, watchlists, or fund lists when one fund needs a dense summary in a Card.
Use `cardType` to match density (large hero vs inline strip).
notes: `fundName` is required; visibility of sub-blocks depends on `cardType`.
Do not use for equity holdings rows—use StocksCard or ListItem stocks variants.
AMC mark uses Avatar `type=logo`.

widgets.name: NewsWidget
widgets.description: Section header plus horizontally scrolling news cards (badges, body, time).
usage: Use below portfolio or discover blocks for headline-style market news.
Use when cards should peek horizontally with consistent card padding tokens.
notes: `onCardAction` fires from the card chevron—not the whole card (verify UX with product).
Set `showBadges=false` to hide all badge chips regardless of `items[].badges`.
Do not use for long-form fund strategy cards—compose SectionHeader + Card + DataPoints + Button instead.

widgets.name: PaymentMethodSelectionWidget
widgets.description: Payment flow step: pick exactly one saved or available instrument (UPI, net banking, card on file, etc.) with primary and secondary lines per row, empty and unavailable fallbacks, and loading.

props.title: string — section title (e.g. Payment method)
props.methods: PaymentMethodRow[] — each { id: string, primaryLabel: string, secondaryLabel?: string }
props.helperText: string — optional guidance under the list
props.unavailableMessage: string — when methods failed to load (partial fetch)

usage: Prefer PaymentMethodSelectionWidget after amount selection and before review in bill pay, checkout, or wallet flows.
Use when exactly one method must be chosen from a list—avoid reassembling Card + ListItem + Radio without a shared selection contract.
Source: `src/components/PaymentMethodSelectionWidget/` — parent owns Continue; pass `emptyMessage` / `unavailableMessage` when APIs return no or failed methods.
notes: Source: `src/components/PaymentMethodSelectionWidget/` — export `PaymentMethodSelectionWidget`, types `PaymentMethodRow`, `PaymentMethodSelectionWidgetProps`.
Exactly **one** row must read as **selected** at a time; `selectedMethodId` must match the highlighted control.
**Primary** and **secondary** metadata (e.g. VPA vs “Primary”) must stay **readable**—no cramped or low-contrast treatment for the subtitle line.
**Continue / review CTA** readiness in the parent flow must depend on a **valid** `selectedMethodId` (non-null when methods exist); do not advance with no selection.
Prefer this contract over ad hoc **card + list + radio** composition so selection, empty, and unavailable states stay consistent with `LedgerActivityListWidget`-style patterns where applicable.

widgets.name: PaymentOutcomeWidget
widgets.description: Post-submit payment outcomes: processing (in-flight), success (confirmed with amount/reference and next steps), or failure (recoverable error with retry and safe exit)—one contract instead of three separate screen fragments.

props.status: processing | success | failure — drives layout and semantics
props.title: string — headline for success/failure; optional or generic for processing
props.message: string — body copy (e.g. bank confirmation timing, error explanation)
props.amountPaid: string — formatted INR line for success; omit or empty when N/A
props.referenceId: string — transaction reference when success; optional
props.supportText: string — secondary line (e.g. where to find receipt, support hint)
props.primaryActionLabel: string — e.g. Done, Retry, View activity
props.secondaryActionLabel: string — optional (e.g. Back home, Contact support)
props.onSecondaryAction: () => void — omit when no secondary CTA
props.loading: boolean — optional extra busy state; must be true or implied when status is processing

usage: Render immediately after the user confirms pay/submit—replace ad hoc full-screen Loading, then swap to this widget (or equivalent) for terminal success/failure on the same route or step.
Use in bill pay, wallet sends, checkout, and UPI/Bank-transfer flows wherever the gateway returns async success, failure, or timeout.
Parent owns routing: map `onPrimaryAction` / `onSecondaryAction` to dismiss, retry payment, open receipts, or exit without implying funds moved on failure.
notes: Source: `src/components/PaymentOutcomeWidget/` — export `PaymentOutcomeWidget`, types `PaymentOutcomeStatus`, `PaymentOutcomeWidgetProps`. Success `DataPoints` render only when both `amountPaid` and `referenceId` are provided.
**Success:** surface `amountPaid` and `referenceId` clearly; `supportText` and CTAs should steer to useful **next actions** (receipts, recent activity, home)—not a dead end.

**Failure:** message must state **no debit** or safe state when applicable; **primary** action should favor **retry** when product allows; **secondary** should offer a **safe exit** (back, change method, home) without trapping the user.
**Processing:** use a dedicated in-flight layout (spinner/progress copy)—must feel **distinct** from terminal success/failure (no celebratory or error chrome, no premature reference IDs).

Prefer this widget contract over scattered **Alert + Loading + DataPoints + Button** blocks so transactional screens stay consistent with `screen-generation-rules.md` and review checklists for outcome clarity and recovery.

widgets.name: PaymentReviewWidget
widgets.description: Final review before submit: restates card/account, paying amount, payment method, optional extra summary rows, disclaimer copy, and an explicit confirmation control (e.g. checkbox) gating the pay action.

props.title: string — e.g. Review & confirm
props.cardLabel: string — card/account identity value (row label in UI is fixed “Card”)
props.summaryRows: ReviewSummaryRow[] — optional extra { label: string, value: string }[]
props.confirmationLabel: string — checkbox label (legal or product acknowledgment)

usage: Prefer PaymentReviewWidget immediately before the irreversible pay/submit step in bill pay, checkout, or transfer flows.
Use when the spec requires restating amount + method + identity and an explicit user acknowledgment—avoid a loose Card plus Checkbox without a shared contract.
Source: `src/components/PaymentReviewWidget/` — confirm/pay CTA stays in the parent screen; gate on `confirmationChecked` and loading/processing flags.
notes: Source: `src/components/PaymentReviewWidget/` — export `PaymentReviewWidget`, types `ReviewSummaryRow`, `PaymentReviewWidgetProps`.
**Critical payment details** (card/account, amount, method) must be **repeated clearly** in the summary—not collapsed into a single vague line.
The **confirmation affordance** (`confirmationLabel` + `confirmationChecked`) must be **explicit**—no implicit consent from tapping Pay alone unless product legal approves.
Parent flow **CTA readiness** (Confirm and pay) must track **review completeness**: typically `confirmationChecked` true and not `loading` / `processing`.
Prefer this contract over ad hoc **summary Card + checkbox** composition so transactional screens meet `screen-generation-rules.md` review and `screen-review-checklist.md` Transaction UX checks.

widgets.name: PortfolioWidget
widgets.description: Portfolio hero: total value, privacy toggle, returns, chart, time-range chips, KPIs, optional buying power.
usage: Use as the first major block on Stock Home after Header/Ticker.
Use when you need privacy masking, range chips, and the bundled GraphWidget in one unit.
notes: Do not wrap in Card—widget is already a full-width hero; use section spacing (`--spacing-56`) outside.
Time-range chips use `Chip` extra-small and space-between row per screen patterns.
Export `maskPortfolioDigits` for consistent masking with the eye toggle.
Do not duplicate GraphWidget below this block for the same series unless the spec shows two charts.

widgets.name: ReminderWidget
widgets.description: Upcoming-action cards: single full-width or horizontal carousel with ListItem + CTA.
usage: Use for incomplete orders, SIP reminders, or actionable alerts that need a primary CTA per row.
Use `variant=carousel` when multiple reminders should peek on the sides.
notes: Carousel uses flex edge spacers—do not remove ReminderWidget.css carousel pattern when changing layout.
Do not use for passive news—use NewsWidget; do not use for full holdings—use StocksCard.
Per-item fields (logo, CTA, close) live on `ReminderItem` in the source file.

widgets.name: Search
widgets.description: Search input styled for chrome: optional search/mic icons, clear, Enter to submit.
usage: Use in Header or discover when the primary action is search, not generic text entry.
Use when you need mic/clear affordances specific to search.
notes: Enter runs `onSubmit`; use TextField for forms, passwords, and floating-label fields.
Do not use inside Card for auth—login/signup use TextField per patterns.
Prefer full width of the content column; avoid squeezing beside many icons without design check.

widgets.name: SectionHeader
widgets.description: Standard section title row with optional chevron, subtext, trailing action, and chip strip.
usage: Use to start any major vertical section (widgets, lists) with consistent 16px-aligned title rhythm.
Use `trailing=link` or `button` for “View all” / secondary actions tied to the section.
notes: Wrap content below in `.sh-section__content` with section spacing per pml-screen-patterns.
`size=extra-large` always shows a chevron.
Do not nest a full Header inside a section—SectionHeader is for in-page sections only.

widgets.name: StocksCard
widgets.description: One holding row inside a Card (standard or MTF margin footer).
usage: Use in portfolio or position lists when each row is one security with qty, avg, LTP, and change.
Use `layout=mtf` when the design shows margin return footer under the row.
notes: Composes ListItem `stocks-card` / `stocks-card-mtf`—prefer this over raw ListItem for holdings cards.
Default layout omits trailing chevron icon; use text/link/button trailing if needed.
INR: no space after ₹ in formatted strings.

widgets.name: Ticker
widgets.description: Horizontal strip of index-style pills: name, price, change, optional expiry badge.
usage: Use directly under Header on Stock Home for NIFTY/SENSEX-style snapshot.
Use when each item is one benchmark row, not arbitrary menu items.
notes: Set `expiryLabel` only on rows that need the expiry Badge; leave others blank for mixed strips.
Horizontal overflow is expected—strip scrolls within the phone column.
Do not use for user-generated lists or settings—wrong information density.

widgets.name: WheelCarousel
widgets.description: Center-snapped infinite horizontal selector for short text tabs.
usage: Use only when you need a wheel-style tab strip without the heatmap (custom filters, experimental layouts).
Prefer HeatmapWidget when sector cards and wheel belong together.
notes: Default `repeatCount` is 51 (odd values work best for infinite scroll).
Do not duplicate the wheel inside HeatmapWidget—that widget already embeds WheelCarousel.
Not a replacement for Tabs under Header—Tabs is underline style; WheelCarousel is pill + snap scroll.

widgets.name: SectionErrorBanner
widgets.description: Section- or page-level error, warning, or recoverable failure message using the sleek or detailed Alert layout with optional primary action (e.g. retry).

props.type: sleek | detailed — compact bar vs titled card
props.state: negative | warning | primary | positive — semantic severity (use negative/warning for failures; primary for neutral notices in error slots)
props.title: string — detailed type only
props.showCta: boolean — show action button (retry, open settings, etc.)
props.className: string — e.g. stack spacing in a banner column

usage: Place at the top of `.sh-section__content`, under a SectionHeader, or in a dedicated notices stack at the top of the scroll region (see CreditCardBillDashboard banner pattern).

Use when a whole section or screen region failed to load, needs a warning before content, or offers a clear recovery action—pair `state="negative"` or `warning` with `showCta` + `onCtaClick` for retry.
Use sleek for dense stacks; detailed when title + body + CTA need stronger hierarchy.
notes: There is no separate `SectionErrorBanner.tsx` export today—this registry entry is the canonical contract: implement with `Alert` from `components/Alert` using the props above until a thin wrapper is added.
Prefer this documented shape over ad hoc divs + raw colors when modeling the same UX so generated screens stay token-safe and consistent with `Alert.css`.
Do not use for field-level validation—use `TextField` / `Dropdown` error props; do not replace transient toasts—consider `Snackbar` for short post-action feedback.
For marketing or one-off copy blocks that are not error/recovery semantics, raw `Alert` with custom `className` is fine; this widget name signals **failure / warning / recoverable** section UI.

widgets.name: CompactQuantityStepperWidget
widgets.description: Implemented single-row − / value / + stepper for dense trading and order-entry: icon-only stroke Buttons + low-emphasis TextField, token-tight `gap: var(--spacing-2)`, shared 32px row height in `CompactQuantityStepperWidget.css`.

props.step: number — increment/decrement delta; +/- snaps with `round(clamp/step)*step` then reclamps
props.disabled: boolean — disables buttons and input; root gets `cqsw--disabled`
props.decrementLabel: string — accessible name for minus action
props.incrementLabel: string — accessible name for plus action
props.onChange: (value: number | string) => void — from +/- emits clamped numeric; from typing emits raw string (parent normalizes)

usage: Prefer CompactQuantityStepperWidget on equity, F&O, and other compact transaction flows whenever the spec shows a single integrated stepper row.
Use instead of hand-building minus/value/plus rows—spacing and neutral step-button styling stay consistent.
With focus in the center field, ArrowUp / ArrowDown increment or decrement (same clamp rules as buttons).
notes: Source: `src/components/CompactQuantityStepperWidget/` — export `CompactQuantityStepperWidget`, type `CompactQuantityStepperWidgetProps`; import from `components/CompactQuantityStepperWidget`.
Decrement at `min` and increment at `max` disable the respective button; root adds `cqsw--min-reached` / `cqsw--max-reached` for optional styling.
Step buttons use widget-scoped neutral stroke (overrides default green stroke Button) so the row reads as one trading control.
Decrement shows a Unicode minus glyph; increment uses `plus_outline` Icon—slight visual asymmetry vs two matching glyphs.
TextField label `Qty` is visually hidden; center value uses `aria-label="Quantity"`. Typed values are not auto-clamped on blur—parent should validate or rewrite `value` if needed.
